Just now

Spring Boot makes it easy to build production-ready applications with minimal configuration. A developer can create a REST API, connect to a database, and deploy an application within minutes. However, the simplicity that Spring Boot offers often hides a significant amount of complexity underneath.
During interviews, especially for mid-level and senior backend positions, the discussion quickly moves beyond annotations and CRUD operations. Interviewers often focus on how Spring Boot works internally and expect candidates to understand the framework’s architecture, startup process, configuration management, and runtime behavior.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by S O C I A L . C U T on UnsplashThe following questions represent some of the most frequently asked Spring Boot internals topics in recent interviews.
1. How Does Spring Boot Decide Which Auto-Configuration to Apply?
Spring Boot’s auto-configuration mechanism is powered by @EnableAutoConfiguration. During startup, Spring Boot scans configuration classes declared in META-INF files and evaluates numerous conditions before applying them.
Conditions can depend on:

• Presence or absence of classes in the classpath

• Existing bean definitions

• Configuration properties
